name: Kentucky Wonder
image_alt: Long green pole beans hanging from a trellis
description: The gold standard of American pole beans. Produces long, flat to slightly rounded pods with a meaty texture and rich, full bean flavor. One of the most productive heirloom beans in cultivation.
history: Introduced in 1864 as 'Texas Pole' and renamed Kentucky Wonder by 1877. Has been continuously popular for 150 years — a testament to its unmatched combination of flavor, productivity, and adaptability.

good.reason: Three Sisters — beans climb corn stalks

seasonal_quirks: Requires warm soil — do not plant until soil reaches 60°F. Produces prolifically through summer with consistent harvesting. Yields drop in extreme heat above 95°F but resume when temperatures moderate.
harvest_cues: Pods should snap cleanly when bent. 6–9 inches long, before seeds develop visible bulges. Check every 2–3 days at peak season.
variety_notes: Can also be grown as a dry shell bean — let pods dry on the vine for brown seeds with excellent storage quality. The dry beans are good in soups.
preservation: Blanch and freeze. Pickle in dill brine. Shell and dry for winter storage.
